skill.name: raffle-winner-picker
skill.description: Selects random winners from lists, CSV files, spreadsheets, or Google Sheets for raffles, giveaways, and participant draws.
skill.summary: Pick fair raffle and giveaway winners from common entry sources.
skill.author: DYAI2025

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported shell execution, weak cryptography, and network reconnaissance patterns, but review found these are false positives in Markdown examples and descriptive text. The skill contains only one documentation file and no executable code, network calls, prompt injection text, or data exfiltration behavior.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static Finding: Markdown Examples Misread as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick locations are Markdown code fences and user prompt examples. They do not execute commands and do not pass user input to a shell.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Every reported location is inside Markdown prose or fenced examples. No script file, command runner, backtick expression, or executable shell context exists in the skill.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static Finding: Weak Cryptography Pattern Is Descriptive Text
low_findings.description: The line describes random winner selection in the skill metadata. It does not implement a random number generator or use a weak cryptographic algorithm.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is only the skill description and contains no code. There is no implemented algorithm to assess as weak or exploitable.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static Finding: Network Reconnaissance Pattern Is Descriptive Text
low_findings.description: The reported line is a bullet point about transparent selection with no network command, scanner, endpoint, or reconnaissance behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is feature documentation, not executable logic. No evidence found for network probing, host discovery, or outbound requests.

content.value_statement: Manual winner selection can create bias and poor records. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code through fair random draws from common entry sources.
actual_capabilities: Guides random winner selection from plain lists, CSV files, spreadsheets, and Google Sheets.
Supports one winner, multiple winners, runner-ups, and duplicate prevention.
Explains weighted selection based on an entries or ticket count column.
Encourages transparent records with timestamps, winner details, and selection method notes.
Supports eligibility exclusions, such as removing previous winners before drawing.
limitations: The skill is guidance only and does not include executable raffle software.
Google Sheets access depends on the host tool permissions and user-provided access.
It cannot independently verify contest rules, identity, or legal eligibility.
Claims about cryptographic randomness depend on the model or tool implementation used.

use_cases.title: Run a social giveaway
use_cases.description: Select one or more winners from exported comments, forms, or subscriber lists.

use_cases.title: Draw event raffle prizes
use_cases.description: Pick winners and runner-ups from attendee spreadsheets while avoiding duplicate winners.

use_cases.title: Assign limited program spots
use_cases.description: Randomly choose participants from eligible applicants and keep a clear selection record.

prompt_templates.title: Pick one winner
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner draw from a pasted list
prompt_templates.prompt: Pick one random winner from this list. Show the total entries, the winner, and the selection timestamp.

prompt_templates.title: Pick multiple winners
prompt_templates.prompt: Pick three random winners from entries.csv. Do not allow the same email address to win twice.

prompt_templates.title: Pick winner and runner-ups
prompt_templates.prompt: Pick one grand prize winner and three runner-ups from this spreadsheet. Exclude anyone marked ineligible.

prompt_templates.title: Run a weighted verified draw
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the entries column as ticket weight, exclude previous winners, and provide a transparent draw summary with timestamp.

output_examples.input: Pick one winner from a list of newsletter subscribers.
output_examples.output: The result lists the total subscriber count, selected winner, entry details, selection method, and timestamp.

output_examples.input: Pick five event raffle winners and three runner-ups from an attendee spreadsheet.
output: A winner list with no duplicate names or email addresses.
A short audit note describing exclusions and timestamp.

output_examples.input: Pick a weighted winner where each ticket count increases the chance of selection.
output_examples.output: The result explains the weighted entry count, selected winner, and how the ticket column affected the draw.

best_practices: Clean the entry list before drawing winners.
Record the timestamp, source file, and selection method.
Pick runner-ups when winner eligibility may need review.
anti_patterns: Do not include private data unless it is needed for the draw.
Do not change eligibility rules after seeing the selected winner.
Do not claim legal compliance without checking local contest rules.

faq.question: Can this skill pick from Google Sheets?
faq.answer: Yes, it can guide a draw from Google Sheets when the host tool can access the sheet.

faq.question: Can it pick more than one winner?
faq.answer: Yes, it supports multiple winners and can avoid duplicate winners.

faq.question: Does it support weighted entries?
faq.answer: Yes, it can use an entries or ticket count column for weighted selection.

faq.question: Can it verify winner eligibility?
faq.answer: It can apply eligibility fields that you provide, but it cannot verify facts independently.

faq.question: Does it store participant data?
faq.answer: The skill itself is documentation and stores no data. Data handling depends on the host tool.

faq.question: Is the draw cryptographically secure?
faq.answer: The skill recommends secure randomness, but actual security depends on the tool performing the draw.
